mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or unemployment rate does not exceed 4.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing starts rate above 1.5 million units signals an expansion in the housing market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or housing starts rate does not exceed 1.5 million units for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 112.5 signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector that reduces the supply of goods and services, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or industrial production index does not decline below 112.5 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real GDP growth rate above 2.5% signals an expansion in the economy that boosts corporate profits and stock prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or real GDP growth rate does not exceed 2.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 207.5 signals an inflationary pressure that reduces the purchasing power of consumers and boosts interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in inflationary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or consumer price index does not exceed 207.5 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the payroll employment rate above 137.5 million signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of skilled workers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or payroll employment rate does not exceed 137.5 million for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real disposable personal income above 377.5 million signals an expansion in consumer spending that boosts stock prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or real disposable personal income does not exceed 377.5 million for 3 consecutive months.
